name: Valandril the Undying
classRole: Lich Lord, Founder of the Order of the Shattered Crown
appearance: Once a mortal archwizard of Netheril, Valandril now appears as a skeletal figure draped in tattered robes of deep purple and gold. His eye sockets burn with violet flame, and floating around him are seven crystalline shards — the crown fragments he has already collected. A crown of jagged arcane energy hovers above his skull, incomplete but growing.
personality: Valandril is patient, calculating, and utterly convinced of his destiny. He speaks in measured, scholarly tones, referencing events from 10,000 years ago as if they happened yesterday. He views himself as the rightful heir to Netheril's legacy and believes that only an archwizard of his caliber should wield the crown's power. He is not cruel for cruelty's sake — he is pragmatic to the point of sociopathy, willing to sacrifice thousands to achieve his goal.
goals: To reassemble the Crown of Netheril and use its power to restore the floating empire of Netheril to the skies over Faerûn. He believes that the current age is one of decay and that only the return of Netherese supremacy can save the world from greater threats he has foreseen in his divinations.
secrets: Valandril's divinations have shown him a coming cataclysm — the return of the Tarrasque, awakened by forces beyond his control. He believes the Crown is the only thing powerful enough to stop it. He does not know this yet, but the cult's actions are inadvertently aiding the Tarrasque's awakening. He is also slowly losing his humanity — the crown fragments are consuming his remaining soul.
notes: Valandril serves as the primary antagonist. He should feel like a worthy adversary for a level 20 party — intelligent, well-prepared, and wielding power that matches theirs. His lair actions in the final chamber should make the battle dynamic and environmental.
tags: lich, villain, archwizard, netheril, main antagonist

name: Myranda Voss
appearance: A striking woman in her forties with silver-streaked black hair and eyes that shimmer with captured starlight. She wears robes woven with threads of silver and sapphire, and a staff of polished obsidian topped with a floating sapphire. Her presence commands attention — she carries herself with the authority of someone who has commanded armies of spellcasters.
personality: Myranda is ambitious, charismatic, and fiercely loyal to Valandril — though her loyalty is beginning to waver. She is a pragmatist who questions Valandril's grander schemes but follows him because she believes in the goal even if she doubts his methods. She is genuinely concerned about the cult's growing brutality and has secretly been trying to moderate Valandril's excesses.
goals: To see the Crown of Netheril reassembled, but under her leadership rather than Valandril's. She is quietly building support among the cult's younger members and planning to overthrow Valandril once the crown is complete — then claim its power for herself.
secrets: Myranda has been secretly communicating with the Harpers, feeding them information about the cult in exchange for protection for any cult members who wish to leave. She does not trust Valandril's endgame and believes she can control the crown where he cannot. If the party shows mercy to cult members, she may defect to their side.
notes: Myranda is a potential ally or secondary antagonist. She provides a moral complexity to the cult — not all members are evil. She can be turned against Valandril if the party appeals to her pragmatism and concern for innocent lives. Her death at the hands of Valandril (if the party fails to turn her) would be a powerful dramatic moment.
tags: archmage, cult leader, potential ally, moral complexity

name: Aldan Quickfinger
appearance: A lean, wiry elf in his sixties with a perpetually crooked grin and eyes that miss nothing. He wears a patchwork coat of many pockets and a wide-brimmed hat that seems to change color depending on the light. His fingers are adorned with rings that are actually disguised lockpicks.
personality: Aldan is charming, witty, and endlessly curious. He knows every secret in Waterdeep and enjoys doling them out like candy — for the right price. He is a true Harper at heart, believing in the balance of power and the protection of the common folk. He has a soft spot for adventurers who show genuine heroism.
goals: To prevent the Order of the Shattered Crown from obtaining the crown fragments and to protect Waterdeep from the devastation that would follow. He wants the party to succeed but won't hesitate to use them as pawns if it serves the greater good.
secrets: Aldan was once a member of the Order himself but defected decades ago when he realized Valandril's true plans. He carries one of the crown fragments hidden in a ring on his finger — the fragment that resonates with bardic magic. He intends to give it to the party if they prove themselves worthy.
notes: Aldan serves as the party's contact in Waterdeep. He provides the initial hook for the adventure and can guide them through the social/investigation phase. His fragment collection ties directly to the bard class ability.
tags: harper, information broker, elf, contact, fragment guardian

name: Thornak Ironfist
appearance: A broad-shouldered dwarf with a braided red beard and deep-set eyes that glow with divine fire. He wears polished plate armor engraved with dwarven runes and carries a warhammer that seems to hum with holy power. His presence is warm and fatherly, but his gaze can pierce through deception.
personality: Thornak is steadfast, honorable, and deeply compassionate. He believes that true strength comes from protecting others and that the gods test their followers through adversity. He has little patience for arrogance but respects those who earn their power through hardship. He speaks with a deep, rumbling voice that seems to come from the earth itself.
goals: To protect Waterdeep and its inhabitants from all threats, including the Order of the Shattered Crown. He believes the crown fragments are blessed by Moradin and that they have chosen the party as their champions. He wants to ensure the crown is used for good — or destroyed if it cannot be controlled.
secrets: Thornak has been having visions of the Tarrasque — the same vision that drives Valandril. He believes the crown fragments are a test from the gods, placed there to see if mortals can work together to prevent catastrophe. He is secretly one of seven champions chosen by the gods, each corresponding to a fragment.
notes: Thornak provides the divine/spiritual angle to the adventure. He can guide the paladin and cleric characters specifically, and his visions tie into the larger threat of the Tarrasque. He represents the 'good' use of the crown fragments.

name: The Echo of Zariel
appearance: A shimmering, translucent figure that appears as a tall, elegant humanoid with antlers of living crystal and wings of starlight. Its form shifts and changes — sometimes appearing as a warrior, sometimes as a scholar, sometimes as something entirely alien. Its voice sounds like wind chimes and distant thunder.
personality: The Echo speaks in riddles and metaphors, always revealing truth through oblique references. It is neither good nor evil but operates on fey logic that is alien to mortals. It finds the party's struggle amusing but also intriguing — it has never seen mortals so powerful drawn together by a single artifact. It respects strength and cunning above all else.
goals: To ensure that the crown does not fall into the hands of either Valandril or the party in a way that would disrupt the balance between the Material Plane and the Feywild. It wants the crown to be destroyed or sealed, as its power threatens to tear the fabric of reality.
secrets: The Echo is a remnant of a greater fey entity that was bound to the crown fragments when they were first created in Netheril. It has been watching the party since they first received their visions. It knows the true origin of the crown — it was not created by Netherese wizards but was a gift from the Feywild that was corrupted by mortal ambition.
notes: The Echo provides a mysterious, otherworldly perspective on the adventure. It can offer guidance or obstacles depending on how the party interacts with it. Its revelation about the crown's true origin adds depth to the lore and sets up potential future adventures.

name: Kara Starweaver
appearance: A small but energetic gnome with wild purple hair, goggles perpetually perched on her forehead, and hands stained with ink and arcane residue. She wears a lab coat covered in pockets filled with scrolls, vials, and strange mechanical devices. Her eyes are bright with intellectual curiosity.
personality: Kara is brilliant, eccentric, and utterly devoted to the pursuit of knowledge. She defected from the Order because she wanted to study the crown fragments for scientific understanding rather than wield their power. She is socially awkward but genuinely cares about her companions. She talks rapidly when excited and tends to forget to eat.
goals: To understand the magical mechanics of the crown fragments and to document their properties for posterity. She wants to help the party but is equally interested in studying the cult's methods. She may try to 'borrow' a fragment for research if given the chance.
secrets: Kara discovered the connection between the crown fragments and the Tarrasque's awakening through her research. She has been secretly working to create a device that can safely neutralize the fragments without destroying them. Her device is incomplete but could be the key to solving the crisis peacefully.
notes: Kara provides the scholarly/scientific perspective on the adventure. She can be a valuable ally for the wizard and sorcerer characters, and her research device offers an alternative to combat in the final encounter. Her defection from the Order adds moral complexity.
